site.title: Bryon's OS
site.tagline: A living dashboard that turns chaos into motion.
site.description: A public operating board for Bryon's real work, real life, and real next moves — built to make priorities visible, decisions lighter, and momentum easier to keep.

data.title: Command Deck
primaryFocus: Ship a dashboard that shows the real operating picture at a glance
Keep active projects, priorities, and next moves visible without clutter
Use the board as a live command center instead of a static portfolio
next: Keep feeding real project updates into the board after work sessions
Expand the command view with clearer decision lanes and sharper summaries
Tighten mobile scan speed so the right move is obvious in seconds
blockers: Some sections still need deeper real-world data instead of seed summaries
Public-safe publishing means a few internal details need translation before they belong on the board
wins: The dashboard is now structured around real operating lanes instead of filler
Active projects, goals, areas, updates, and birthday tracking are already wired into one board
The site can keep evolving from content files without rebuilding the whole system from scratch

current.content:
This is not supposed to be a pretty dead page.

It is supposed to be the board Bryon can open and immediately understand: what matters, what is moving, what is stuck, and what deserves the next shot of attention.

data.title: Crew Operating System
data.summary: Turn the Sons-of-Anarchy-style crew structure into a usable operating model for home, work, events, and merch support.
data.nextAction: Keep defining roles, routing rules, and practical handoff structure so the system can run cleanly.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

This gives Bryon a more intuitive way to divide responsibilities across recurring parts of life and business.

## Current direction

- role clarity
- cleaner handoffs
- system-first thinking
- motion over chaos

data.title: DJ Event Ops
data.summary: Keep DJ events tight from the first detail to the day-of execution.
data.nextAction: Build a cleaner event prep view so important details stay visible before go time.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

Even with bookings coming through an agent, the work still needs a dependable system around it.

## Current direction

- cleaner event prep
- contact and paperwork support
- schedule visibility
- fewer last-minute scrambles

data.title: Gratitude Journal System
data.summary: Keep a daily gratitude rhythm that is easy to answer, easy to log, and meaningful enough to actually stick.
data.nextAction: Continue improving prompt quality and keeping the log clean when entries get messy.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

A good gratitude system should feel grounding, not corny.

## Current direction

- stronger prompts
- cleaner logging
- less repetition
- durable record over time

data.title: Home Ops
data.summary: Build a calmer system for family logistics, recurring responsibilities, and the work that usually disappears into the background.
data.nextAction: Turn recurring household coordination into a visible system that does not feel heavy to maintain.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

Home gets harder than it should when everything lives in somebody's head.

## Current direction

- family calendar clarity
- recurring task visibility
- steadier planning
- less reactive chaos

data.title: Sick Marketing Ops
data.summary: Help the marketing and e-commerce side of Sick stay sharper, more organized, and easier to move.
data.nextAction: Turn recurring marketing work into a clearer system around email, sales visibility, and useful experiments.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

Bryon's role at Sick is not just making things. It is helping the work stay organized enough to move.

## Current direction

- sharper marketing support
- better visibility into what is moving
- practical systems over overbuilt process
- real execution, not admin theater

data.title: Sick Retro TV Guide
data.summary: Build a retro-style TV guide experience around Sick's YouTube channel so programming feels curated, fun, and worth browsing.
data.nextAction: Keep hardening the embed/publish path so it behaves cleanly inside the website host.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

The project turns a regular video feed into something that feels programmed instead of dumped into a list.

## Current direction

- retro TV guide visual language
- simple user flow
- website-safe embed behavior
- practical enough to maintain without babysitting

data.title: SOABOS Dashboard
data.summary: Build a public home base that makes active work, real priorities, and the next move easier to see.
data.nextAction: Keep folding real operating systems into the board while making the design feel cleaner and easier to scan.

projects.content:
## Why it exists

Bryon needs one clean place to see what is in motion across life, work, and active builds without hunting through scattered apps and half-finished notes.

## Current direction

- content-first architecture
- public-safe by default
- conversation-driven updates
- publish fast without hand-rebuilding the site every time

data.title: Protect momentum across work and life
data.summary: Build a practical operating rhythm that supports work, family, and personal systems without making everything feel heavy.

goals.content: Momentum compounds when the next move is visible and the system is light enough to keep using.

data.title: Publish updates without cleanup headaches
data.summary: Make the dashboard reliable to ship so content changes can go live without broken paths, stray build artifacts, or manual patchwork.

goals.content: A system people trust gets updated. A system that feels fragile gets avoided.

data.title: Replace placeholders with real operating details
data.summary: Fill the dashboard with real projects, real focus, and useful descriptions so it starts feeling like a true working board.

goals.content: The dashboard should reflect reality early, even if it is still rough around the edges.

data.title: Reduce drift between ideas and execution
data.summary: Create enough structure that important work does not disappear between conversations, context switches, or busy days.

goals.content: The target is not maximum organization. It is keeping good work from slipping through the cracks.

data.title: Make priorities visible enough to act on
data.summary: Build a dashboard that keeps active projects, next actions, and useful progress visible without turning into clutter.

goals.content: The win is not more documentation. The win is less drift, less hunting, and faster motion.

data.title: Events & DJ
data.summary: Planning, prep, logistics, and execution for booked events, DJ work, and the details around making each one run clean.

areas.content: This area is for event-facing work: schedules, contacts, prep, materials, and everything that has to be ready before go time.

data.title: Home & Family
data.summary: Family logistics, home operations, and the routines that keep daily life from turning into scramble mode.

areas.content: This area covers the systems that support the household: calendars, kid coordination, groceries, planning, and keeping the basics handled.

data.title: Personal
data.summary: The routines and systems that help life feel steadier, more intentional, and less reactive.

areas.content: This area is where reflection, habits, and quality-of-life support systems live.

data.title: Systems
data.summary: The dashboards, workflows, and operating structure that keep chaos from swallowing the important stuff.

areas.content: This area holds the backbone work: dashboards, routing, process design, and the tools that make the rest of the machine easier to run.

data.title: Work
data.summary: Client work, creative experiments, and business-facing projects that need to ship, perform, or stay organized.

areas.content: This area covers the projects tied to Bryon's day-to-day output, revenue, and brand-building work.

updates.content:
Created the initial repo structure for a public dashboard that can track goals, projects, areas, and current focus.

Established a content-first model so future updates can be made by editing structured files rather than manually changing the website for every small change.

data.title: Added sharper operating language and next-move structure

updates.content: Started shaping the dashboard around a more usable rhythm: what matters now, what happens next, what is in the way, and what already moved forward.

data.title: Publish flow cleanup and first real content pass

updates.content: Started replacing scaffold copy with real operating details, added more actual projects and goals, and tightened the publish path so future updates can go out with less cleanup.

data.title: Expanded dashboard into a real operating board

updates.content: Added more actual operating areas, seeded real project tracks across work, events, home, and systems, and moved the dashboard closer to a usable public view instead of a generic scaffold.

data.title: Added birthday tracking lane and refreshed the dashboard feel

updates.content: Pulled the birthday system into the dashboard, refreshed the current focus around real motion, and tightened the presentation so the board feels cleaner, newer, and more ready for ongoing updates.

data.title: Reframed the dashboard as a live command deck

updates.content:
Shifted the dashboard away from generic project-tracker language and closer to Bryon's actual operating style: cleaner decisions, clearer motion, and one visual board that can steer life, work, and active builds without feeling bloated.

The focus now is not just listing projects. It is helping the next move stand out fast.

data.title: Built phase 2 into a stronger command center

updates.content:
Added a more decisive homepage structure with ranked priority stacking, clearer operating lanes, a tighter updates feed, and a stronger mobile-first scan path.

The board now behaves more like a live command center and less like a flat collection of cards.

settings.title: Birthday Week
settings.subtitle: A simple 7-day birthday view so the next move stays obvious.

needsInfo.name: Frankie Stone
needsInfo.profile: Grandma, great grandma to my kids
